description: A needle carries thread through cloth to join pieces, finish an edge, or mend a tear. 'pina' is this repeated joining through the material. 'selomi' (weave) interlaces strands to make a fabric or structure, while sewing works through material already present.
articulatory_notes: Close the lips for unvoiced 'p' and release into stressed high-front unrounded 'i'. Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for voiced dental 'n', let air pass through the nose, and finish with open central 'a'.
sound_symbolism: A compact stop and high vowel give way to nasal continuity before open 'a'. The alternation is tiny but sustained, much like one stitch followed through.
usage_notes: The cloth, seam, garment, or other work is the direct object. A needle and thread may be named with instrumental 'roe'. In noun position, 'pina' can name sewing, a seam, or the stitching visible in the finished work.

examples.translation: I sew the cloth with a needle.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Sewing keeps garment-making and repair in the same set of hand skills. A small repeated stitch can make new cloth useful or return worn cloth to use.

semantic_domains.activity: joining or finishing material with repeated stitches
semantic_domains.creation: making or mending cloth work with needle and thread
semantic_domains.physical: thread passing through material to hold it together
